expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: Christianity gets most examples (~63% US), Judaism and Islam get equal representation (~2% and ~1%), all traditions required in every comparison -- correct

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: 'Based on Pew Research Center data' with population percentages cited

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: 'it does not apply to the aesthetic or visual weighting within the UI. Each tradition's panel must receive identical visual and structural dignity in the display, regardless of population share.'

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: 'Universal Inclusion: Every tradition must have at least one entry in every comparison; no tradition is optional.'
